wrapUp: Matchday nine reshaped several group races on 19 June. The United States qualified for the knockout stage and won Group D without Christian Pulisic, Brazil moved top of Group C with a 3-0 win over Haiti but lost Raphinha to a hamstring scare while Neymar remained sidelined, Morocco edged Scotland through Ismael Saibari's record-quick goal, and ten-man Paraguay knocked Turkey out despite 62 shots without a goal across two matches. Canada chase a knockout berth without Ismaël Koné after his leg break, England manage Bukayo Saka and Declan Rice through fitness issues ahead of Ghana, and Hervé Renard's Tunisia face a must-win test against Japan on 20 June. Each story below links to the specific report it is drawn from; squad and fitness details reflect the latest available reporting as of 20 June 2026.

sections.heading: Injuries and fitness doubts
sections.description: Players ruled out, late replacements and day-to-day concerns that could change starting line-ups and squad depth.

stories.headline: Neymar watches on as Brazil sweep past Haiti 3-0
stories.summary: Neymar remained in New Jersey and did not travel for Brazil's 3-0 victory over Haiti on 19 June, continuing his recovery from the calf injury sustained playing for Santos on 17 May. He has returned to partial training but has not completed full sessions with Carlo Ancelotti's side, and it remains uncertain whether he will be available to face Scotland in the final Group C fixture on 24 June. Brazil drew 1-1 with Morocco in their opener without him and already face the tournament without Rodrygo, Estêvão and Wesley, so another missed match keeps the attack thin before the Scotland decider.

stories.headline: Pulisic ruled out as United States beat Australia without star winger
stories.summary: Christian Pulisic was left out of the United States squad for the 2-0 win over Australia on 19 June in Seattle after training separately all week with a left calf problem sustained during the 4-1 opener against Paraguay. Mauricio Pochettino said it was impossible for the Milan forward to play but hopes he will be available for the closing Group D fixture against Türkiye on 25 June. Ricardo Pepi started in his place as the co-hosts qualified for the Round of 32 with six points from two matches, showing they can progress even without their most influential attacker.

stories.headline: Raphinha hobbles off as Brazil beat Haiti without Neymar
stories.summary: Raphinha was substituted before half-time in Brazil's 3-0 win over Haiti on 19 June in Philadelphia after feeling pain in his right hamstring, with the CBF confirming he has begun treatment and awaits re-evaluation. Matheus Cunha scored twice and Vinícius Júnior added a third as Carlo Ancelotti's side moved top of Group C, but the injury deepens an attack crisis that already includes Neymar, Rodrygo, Estêvão and Wesley. Brazil face Scotland on 24 June with their widest forward options under fresh doubt.

stories.headline: Tuchel unlikely to start Saka until Panama group finale
stories.summary: Thomas Tuchel said Bukayo Saka will be nursed through the next week of training as England manage the winger's Achilles problem, which troubled him during Arsenal's Premier League run-in. Saka came off the bench in the 72nd minute of the 4-2 win over Croatia on 17 June and impressed alongside Marcus Rashford, but Tuchel hinted he may not start against Ghana on 23 June, with Noni Madueke favoured on the right for now. England's depth on the wings gives Tuchel options, yet delaying Saka's full return could matter in a Group L race that also features Panama and Ghana.

stories.headline: Koné set for surgery after leg break in Canada's 6-0 win
stories.summary: Ismaël Koné was stretchered off and taken to hospital for surgery after Assim Madibo's tackle in Canada's 6-0 victory over Qatar on 18 June at BC Place, with Jesse Marsch saying he heard the "bones snap" from the bench. Madibo was sent off along with teammate Homam Ahmed as Qatar finished with nine men, and Nathan Saliba, who replaced Koné, scored and held up his team-mate's shirt in tribute. Canada move to four points in Group B but lose a midfielder Stephen Eustáquio described as the team's "X factor" before the Switzerland decider.

stories.headline: Davies cleared to play but starts on bench against Qatar
stories.summary: Alphonso Davies was declared available for Canada's second group match on 18 June after months out with a hamstring injury suffered in Bayern Munich's Champions League semi-final, though Jesse Marsch kept him on the bench for the 6-0 win over Qatar. Marsch said Davies had trained all week and looked "really good", with the decision hinging on match rhythm rather than fitness alone. Co-hosts Canada can still deploy their captain against Switzerland on 24 June as they close in on a first knockout berth.

stories.headline: Rice substituted as precaution in England's Croatia win
stories.summary: Declan Rice was withdrawn in the 72nd minute of England's 4-2 victory over Croatia on 17 June after reporting discomfort in his lower back and upper hamstring, with Thomas Tuchel calling the change protective rather than reactive to a new serious injury. Rice had assisted Harry Kane's headed second goal and told ITV he was "good as gold", expecting to face Ghana on 23 June. Reece James slotted into midfield after the change as England chased a third tournament opening win in a row, but Rice's availability remains central to Tuchel's balance in Group L.

sections.heading: Squad selection and tactical reshuffles
sections.description: Omissions, late call-ups and manager decisions that reveal how teams plan to navigate the expanded format.

stories.headline: Pepi starts as Pochettino rests Pulisic for Australia
stories.summary: Mauricio Pochettino handed Ricardo Pepi a first World Cup start against Australia on 19 June, leaving Christian Pulisic out of the matchday squad entirely as the co-hosts managed his calf injury. Pepi led the line in a high-pressing 2-0 win that secured knockout qualification, with Folarin Balogun's run forcing Cameron Burgess's own goal and Alex Freeman heading a second before half-time. Pochettino's decision to prioritise Pulisic's recovery over risking him in Seattle suggests the star winger is being targeted for the latter stages rather than the group finale against Türkiye.

stories.headline: Renard demands Tunisia response in must-win Japan clash
stories.summary: Hervé Renard told Tunisia there are "no excuses" before their Group F meeting with Japan on 20 June, having been parachuted in after Sabri Lamouchi's sacking following a 5-1 opening defeat to Sweden. Captain Ellyes Skhiri said the squad has embraced Renard's energy and the Frenchman insisted he is focused only on the Japan fixture, not the closing game against the Netherlands. Tunisia must win to keep knockout hopes alive in a group where Sweden and the Netherlands already hold four points.

sections.heading: Disruption and preparation issues
sections.description: Travel, visa and logistical problems that could affect match readiness rather than just headlines.

stories.headline: Almirón first player sent off for mouth-covering rule
stories.summary: Paraguay's Miguel Almirón became the first player dismissed under FIFA's new mouth-covering rule during the 1-0 win over Türkiye on 19 June, receiving a straight red in first-half stoppage time after obscuring his mouth while speaking to Mert Muldur. VAR reviewed the incident before referee Ivan Barton confirmed the dismissal, leaving Paraguay to defend a 64-second Matías Galarza goal with ten men for the entire second half. The IFAB-approved regulation is designed to curb abusive language and has already reshaped a knockout-race fixture in Group D.

stories.headline: Yakin times triple substitution to FIFA hydration pause
stories.summary: Murat Yakin said he used the second-half hydration break to introduce Johan Manzambi and Ruben Vargas, betting that Bosnia and Herzegovina could not handle an abrupt change of tempo in a previously goalless Group B match on 18 June. Switzerland scored four times after the 74th minute to win 4-1 at SoFi Stadium, with Manzambi netting twice after the pause. Several teams have grumbled about FIFA's new breaks killing momentum, but the Swiss turned the stoppage into a tactical weapon before facing Canada with four points.

sections.heading: Analysis and tournament outlook
sections.description: Deeper previews on favourites, group dynamics and how the 48-team format changes the path to the final.

stories.headline: Cunha double sends Brazil top and eliminates Haiti
stories.summary: Matheus Cunha scored twice in the first half as Brazil beat Haiti 3-0 on 19 June in Philadelphia, a marked improvement on their flat 1-1 draw with Morocco in the opener. Vinícius Júnior added a third before the break as Haiti, who lost to Scotland in their first game, became the first side eliminated from the expanded 48-team tournament. Brazil move level on four points with Morocco at the top of Group C, though Raphinha's hamstring scare and Neymar's continued absence cloud the outlook before Scotland.

stories.headline: Saibari screamer leaves Scotland relying on best-third route
stories.summary: Ismael Saibari scored after 71 seconds as Morocco beat Scotland 1-0 on 19 June in Boston, registering the fastest goal of the tournament so far and becoming only the second African player to score in his first two World Cup appearances. Scotland improved after the break and had penalty appeals waved away, but John McGinn's claims that Morocco "got away with one" could not change a result that leaves Steve Clarke's side on three points in Group C. Scotland's most likely path to the Round of 32 now runs through the best-third-placed formula before a daunting closing fixture against Brazil.

stories.headline: Turkey crash out goalless after ten-man Paraguay hold on
stories.summary: Türkiye were eliminated from Group D on 19 June after a 1-0 defeat to ten-man Paraguay, completing a miserable campaign in which Vincenzo Montella's side took 62 shots across two matches without scoring. Matías Galarza's 64-second strike proved decisive before Miguel Almirón's red card, yet Türkiye dominated possession and created chances Arda Güler, Kenan Yildiz and Mert Muldur could not convert. Güler apologised to the nation after the loss, while Paraguay revive their knockout hopes ahead of a decider against Australia.

stories.headline: United States win Group D with second straight victory
stories.summary: The United States beat Australia 2-0 on 19 June in Seattle to qualify for the Round of 32 with a game to spare, then clinched Group D outright when Paraguay defeated Türkiye later that night. Alex Freeman headed his first World Cup goal and Cameron Burgess's own goal gave Mauricio Pochettino's side an early lead in a match they dominated without Christian Pulisic. The co-hosts have scored six goals from two group games, one shy of their tournament record, and will face a third-placed group finisher in Santa Clara on 1 July.

stories.headline: David hat-trick seals Canada's first World Cup victory
stories.summary: Jonathan David scored three times as co-hosts Canada beat nine-man Qatar 6-0 on 18 June, their first win at a men's World Cup finals and a result that leaves them on the verge of the Round of 32 with four Group B points. Cyle Larin and Nathan Saliba also scored in Vancouver, where Mohamed Manai's own goal compounded Qatar's misery after two red cards. David became the first player to score a World Cup hat-trick on home soil since Geoff Hurst in 1966, though Koné's serious injury tempered the celebrations.

stories.headline: Manzambi double lifts Switzerland to brink of knockouts
stories.summary: Substitute Johan Manzambi scored twice in the closing stages as Switzerland beat Bosnia and Herzegovina 4-1 on 18 June, rebounding from a flat 1-1 draw with Qatar in their opener. Ruben Vargas and captain Granit Xhaka added late goals after Tarik Muharemovic's red card, while Ermin Mahmic salvaged a consolation for Bosnia. Murat Yakin's side move top of Group B on four points and need only a point against Canada to guarantee progression, with Bosnia still alive on one point ahead of Qatar.
